title: Stelis arcuata
intro: Stelis arcuata, commonly known as Arcuate Stelis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Stelis arcuata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Stelis arcuata is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
taxonomy: Stelis arcuata is classified as a species within the genus Stelis of the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Maxillarieae. The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as orchids. The classification of Stelis arcuata reflects its evolutionary relationships within this group.
